l1: Are permissions missing on files, folders, or Active Directory objects?
When was the unauthorized access attempt or change first observed?
Is the access issue impacting a specific user, group, or service account?
l2: Is Advanced Security Audit Policy configuration applied domain-wide?
Are explicit or inherited ACEs blocking the necessary security context?
Has the AdminSDHolder object modified permissions on protected groups?

l1: Run 'icacls <path>' to view the NTFS discretionary access control list (DACL).
Run 'acldiag <object_DN>' to check permissions on an Active Directory object.
Check the Security event log for Event IDs 4663 (Object Access) or 4670 (Permissions Changed).
l2: Run 'auditpol /get /category:*' to display current active audit policy rules.
Execute 'dsacls <object_DN>' to check explicit and inherited security descriptors.
Search for Event ID 4738 to identify who modified sensitive account permissions.

l1: Enable permission inheritance on a folder via the advanced security settings UI.
Force an audit policy refresh across the domain using 'gpupdate /force'.
l2: Reset inherited ACEs on files using 'icacls <path> /reset /t /c'.
Restore default Active Directory security descriptors using 'dsacls <DN> /resetDefaultDACL'.
Fix AdminSDHolder sync blocks by manually correcting the adminCount attribute.

l1: Export the local NTFS permissions to a text file using 'icacls <path> /save'.
Export the Security event log as a .evtx file from the target server.
Collect the output of the local audit policy status using 'auditpol /get /category:*'.
l2: Export full AD object security descriptors to a CSV using LDP.exe or PowerShell.
Capture a continuous Process Monitor boot log to trace denied file system access.

inherited_permissions_blocked.title: Resolving Access Denied Errors Caused by Blocked NTFS Inheritance

steps.expect: Output displays specific explicit ACEs, but parent permissions are missing
Returns 'True', confirming inheritance has been explicitly disabled/blocked
Command replaces explicit rules with inherited rules from the parent folder
Output shows proper inherited flags (I) matching the parent directory structure
